title: Roget Jiriss

{{Quote|I wouldn't say I'm the best … but the best know who I am and they're worried!|Roget Jiriss|<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" />}}
'''Roget Jiriss''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] and one of the most successful [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] in the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Brash and confident, Jiriss had an intense hatred of all [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]. She always delivered cargo on [[Time/Legends|time]] when agreeing to a job, and her high delivery rate came with correspondingly high fees. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Jiriss's underworld contacts within several large crime syndicates proved an excellent source of information for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. By [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had charged Jiriss with several smuggling-related criminal offenses.

==Biography==
[[File:G11jiriss.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Roget Jiriss, 10 ABY]]
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] Roget Jiriss<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" /> was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="Date" /> As a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], she<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" /> used her contacts deep within several large crime syndicates<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> to garner a certain amount of personal freedom, even in [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]].<ref name="Rebellion Guide">[[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Rebellion'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> Jiriss thus served as an excellent source of information for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" />

Jiriss was later one of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] most successful smugglers in the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and her fees were priced to match<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|166|Jiriss, Roget}}</ref> her high delivery rate. Although Jiriss refused to deal directly with various syndicates, she would quickly accept cargo from a known [[black marketeer]] if the opportunity presented itself in order to hurriedly get out of a [[Star system/Legends|system]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" />

By [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> the Empire had charged Jiriss with illegal [[Transport/Legends|transportation]] of weapons and contraband goods, violations of customs ordinances, and assault on [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police]]. She was categorized in Imperial records as criminal [[TracSheet]] Subject 66436335-UQFC-244869.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" />

==Personality and traits==
Roget Jiriss was slender, with an athletic physique, and stood 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. She had [[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]; waist-length black [[Hair/Legends|hair]], which she kept arranged in a complex pattern;<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" /> and light skin.<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" />

Jiriss was a smuggler with a sense of humor.<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> She was a brash and cocky woman,<ref name="CSWE" /> though her Imperial TracSheet described her as having an exaggerated estimation of her personal skills. Jiriss never made two consecutive smuggling runs in the same system,<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" /> but when she agreed to deliver a shipment, it always arrived on [[Time/Legends|time]].<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> Jiriss had a consuming hatred of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] of any kind and would often break away to "go hunting" when they were reported nearby.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" />

Jiriss's first name, "Roget," was a common [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] female name.<ref name="Gamer 9">{{GamerCite|9|Callsigns: Names of Corellia}}</ref>

==Skills and abilities==
Among her [[Technician/Legends|mechanical abilities]], Jiriss was adept at [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogation]], [[Starship/Legends|starship]] transports, ship [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]], and ship [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]]. She had technical prowess in [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and space transport repair. Aside from her smuggling business proficiency, Jiriss also had knowledge in [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], [[Language/Legends|languages]], and planetary systems.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" />

==Equipment==
Jiriss's personal starship was a heavily armed and modified [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] named the ''[[Frontier Hawk]]''. Her personal equipment included a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" /> She commonly dressed in a blue [[Jumpsuit/Legends|jumpsuit]] with a white collar, which she wore unfastened about her neck,<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" /><ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> along with a [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]] and [[Limb/Legends|fingerless]] work [[Glove/Legends|gloves]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" />

==Behind the scenes==
Roget Jiriss was created for the [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 11: Criminal Organizations]]'', written by [[Rick D. Stuart]]. She serves as a character example of a criminal smuggler for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. Artist [[Mike Vilardi]] provided ''Galaxy Guide 11''{{'s}} illustration of Jiriss.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" />

Jiriss was later included in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|PC game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] as one of the playable characters available to the Rebel Alliance.<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> In accordance with her smuggler contacts, the Jiriss character is used most effectively in gameplay in [[Spy/Legends|espionage]] missions and instigating uprisings on Imperial planets.<ref name="Rebellion Guide" />

The article ''[[Callsigns: Names of Corellia]]'', published in the [[2002]] [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] magazine [[Star Wars Gamer 9|''Star Wars Gamer'' 9]], included "Roget" among a list of common Corellian first names.<ref name="Gamer 9" /> However, Jiriss is not specifically identified as being Corellian.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 11" /><ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /><ref name="Rebellion Guide" /><ref name="CSWE" />
